question.title: Average Order Value by Customer
question.desc: Find the average order value for each customer who has placed more than one order. Join the customers table with the orders table, group by customer name, and calculate the average order value.
question.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS customers; CREATE TABLE customers (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255)); INSERT INTO customers (name) VALUES ('John Doe'), ('Jane Doe'), ('Bob Smith'); DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders; CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER, order_value DECIMAL(10, 2), FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id)); INSERT INTO orders (customer_id, order_value) VALUES (1, 100.00), (1, 200.00), (2, 50.00), (3, 150.00), (3, 250.00), (3, 300.00);
question.solution: SELECT c.name, AVG(o.order_value) AS avg_order_value FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 1
question.tips: Consider using window functions for more complex aggregations.
hints: Use a JOIN to combine the customers and orders tables.
Group by customer name and calculate the average order value using the AVG function.
Add a HAVING clause to filter customers with more than one order.

tests.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS customers; CREATE TABLE customers (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255)); INSERT INTO customers (name) VALUES ('Alice Johnson'), ('Mike Brown'); DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders; CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER, order_value DECIMAL(10, 2), FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id)); INSERT INTO orders (customer_id, order_value) VALUES (1, 75.00), (1, 125.00), (2, 225.00), (2, 325.00);

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS customers; CREATE TABLE customers (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255)); INSERT INTO customers (name) VALUES ('Emily Davis'), ('David Lee'), ('Sarah Taylor'); DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders; CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER, order_value DECIMAL(10, 2), FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id)); INSERT INTO orders (customer_id, order_value) VALUES (1, 90.00), (1, 110.00), (2, 140.00), (3, 160.00), (3, 180.00);
